# UBSAN ignorelist -- known-benign integer sanitizer findings
# Used with -fsanitize-ignorelist= (compile-time suppression)
# Reference: https://clang.llvm.org/docs/SanitizerSpecialCaseList.html
#
# These are NOT bugs. They are correct code that -fsanitize=integer
# flags because the integer sanitizer catches well-defined but
# potentially surprising unsigned arithmetic.
#
# See also: issue #763 for vptr runtime suppression pattern

# RFC 1321 MD5 uses intentional unsigned 32-bit wrapping arithmetic
# and bit rotation via left shift -- all well-defined for unsigned types
# Fires in: IccMD5.cpp MD5Transform() lines 185-254
[unsigned-integer-overflow]
fun:*MD5Transform*

[unsigned-shift-base]
fun:*MD5Transform*

# UTF-16 to UTF-8 encoding appends unsigned char (0x80-0xFF) to std::string
# Sign change from unsigned char to signed char is expected for multi-byte UTF-8
# Fires in: IccTagBasic.cpp CIccLocalizedUnicode::GetText() lines 7238-7253
[implicit-integer-sign-change]
fun:*CIccLocalizedUnicode*GetText*

# libstdc++ std::string comparison subtracts lengths while ordering object keys.
# This is well-defined unsigned arithmetic, but -fsanitize=integer reports it
# through nlohmann::json's std::map-backed object storage.
[unsigned-integer-overflow]
src:*/bits/basic_string.h
